fullArticle:
Tom Hardy — yes, that Tom Hardy — once rapped under the moniker Tommy No 1 before making it big in Hollywood. In recent years, he quietly teamed up with the underground supergroup CZARFACE as Frankie Pulitzer/FacePuller for a handful of songs, and now they’re doing a full project together.

Titled Czarface Meets Frankie Pulitzer, the collaborative album will drop on August 28th via Silver Age. Besides Hardy and the trio of Wu-Tang Clan’s Inspectah Deck with Boston stalwarts 7L & Esoteric, the project features guest appearances from hip-hop royalty such as Busta Rhymes, Method Man, and El-P.

On the lead single, “Brothers Grimm,” Hardy has the unenviable task of following in the footsteps of a lead-off verse from all-time great Inspectah Deck and Esoteric dropping effortless sports referenc...
